title: Best Computer Network & Cyber Security College
content: This programme was developed by the School of Computing Science and Engineering to meet the demand for qualified graduates who can meet the cyber security requirements of mission-critical corporate networks. If you want to move forward in your current profession or switch to a new one, this degree is a great choice. Students may expect to learn the basics of computer science, mathematical reasoning, and ICT in these beginning courses. If you want to learn how to set up computers and set up new hardware, this is the best school for you. Complex network design and management skills will also be covered. At the course's conclusion, students will have gained an understanding of foundational principles in computer science, including safe programming practices, network security, cryptography, internet and web programming, online services, authentication and security models, and web application security. Electives provide students the chance to focus on areas of interest beyond the core curriculum. Therefore, the research is focused on both the theoretical and practical elements of cyber security across several crucial areas. These areas include network security, vulnerability assessment, information security, and the security of wireless devices. The course content is geared on teaching students how to defend a local area network (LAN) or wide area network (WAN) and its associated computers against intrusion, as well as how to recognise and analyse risks, deal with attacks, and develop security policies, procedures, and standards. For Lateral Entry: Passed Diploma examination with at least 60% marks in any branch of Engineering and Technology. Or Passed B.Voc/3-year D.Voc. Stream in the same or allied sector. 1) B.E./B.Tech Ist year minimum 60% marks or equivalent 2) 50% PCM or above at XII level. 01 02 03 01 02 For Lateral Entry: Passed Diploma examination with at least 60% marks in any branch of Engineering and Technology.
